label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage to understand how animals are divided into two kinds. The passage starts by saying, "Animals like the lion are known as carnivorous (meat-eating) animals. They feed on smaller animals. But many other animals eat grass or plants. They are called herbivorous (plant-eating) animals." So the passage directly explains that animals are divided into two groups: carnivorous (meat-eaters) and herbivorous (plant-eaters). Now let's analyze the options: (A) carnivorous animals and meat-eating animals - This option is incorrect because it's redundant. Carnivorous animals are the same as meat-eating animals. The passage doesn't divide animals into two distinct groups where one is a synonym of the other. (B) herbivorous animals and plant-eating animals - This option is also incorrect for the same reason as (A). Herbivorous animals and plant-eating animals are synonyms, so they wouldn't form two distinct categories. (C) carnivorous animals and herbivorous animals - This option matches exactly with the passage. The passage divides animals into two distinct categories: carnivorous (meat-eaters) and herbivorous (plant-eaters).
